title: New partnership aims to accelerate maritime supply chain decarbonization
summary: Yang Ming projects a 63.32% carbon intensity reduction by end-2025 against 2008, a forward-looking claim lacking current, independently verified fleet emission data or actual HFO usage figures. Their EcoSea+ service and PSA partnership pivot on a "Book-and-Claim" framework, a common mechanism for accounting adjustments rather than direct, physical decarbonization at sea. Real progress demands tangible cuts in operational emissions and improved CII ratings, not merely tokenized inset packages designed to manage customer Scope 3 liabilities.
content:
Yang Ming is expanding its decarbonization efforts through new green transport initiatives aimed at reducing supply chain emissions, following a significant reduction in its carbon intensity. The shipping company reported that it had cut its carbon intensity by 63.32% by the end of 2025 compared with 2008 levels, as part of its ongoing efforts to improve energy efficiency and reduce emissions across its operations.

Furthermore, Yang Ming has launched its EcoSea+ service, which uses the company’s low-carbon shipping capabilities to help customers reduce their Scope 3 transportation emissions through a more flexible and transparent approach.

Through its partnership with PSA International, Yang Ming aims to extend the impact of its sustainability efforts beyond ocean transport by integrating maritime and landside solutions.

In addition, PSA is advancing its Node to Network strategy, combining port operations and supply chain capabilities to support greener terminal and inland logistics networks and reduce emissions across the entire supply chain.

Enhancing green competitiveness is at the heart of Yang Ming’s operations. As no single company can achieve supply chain decarbonization alone, we are pleased to partner with PSA, to provide sea-land joint carbon inset package. By combining our maritime fuel initiatives with PSA’s electrified terminal capabilities, we offer global clients an adaptable and verifiable approach to mitigate transparent emissions and fulfil their sustainability objectives.

…said Kevin Lee, President of Yang Ming Marine Transport Corporation.

Beyond emissions measurement and verification, the collaboration focuses on a digital Book-and-Claim framework and a joint maritime-land based inset token package.

This synergy aims to provide cargo stakeholders with a transparent and accountable sea-land pathway to achieve their decarbonization targets.

As responsible stewards of tomorrow, PSA is committed to delivering sustainable impact across the global port and supply chain ecosystem. Tackling the challenges arising from climate change will require the collective efforts of all players in the maritime supply chain sector. We are excited to partner Yang Ming on the decarbonization of global supply chains and support the transition towards a more sustainable global economy.

…commented Ong Kim Pong, PSA International Group CEO.

As stated, the agreement was officially signed by Ivan Chiang, Chief Logistics Officer & Senior Vice President of Yang Ming, and Eddy Ng, Group Head of Operations, Technology and Sustainability of PSA International.

The ceremony concluded successfully in the presence of Kevin Lee, President of Yang Ming Marine Transport Corporation, and Ong Kim Pong, Group CEO of PSA International, marking a milestone in the partners’ shared sustainability journey to advance end-to-end supply chain decarbonization.
